2026-04-13T02:17:36.7251439Z You are in 'detached HEAD' state. You can look around, make experimental
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7251901Z changes and commit them, and you can discard any commits you make in this
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7252318Z state without impacting any branches by switching back to a branch.
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7252553Z 
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7252715Z If you want to create a new branch to retain commits you create, you may
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253103Z do so (now or later) by using -c with the switch command. Example:
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253322Z 
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253424Z   git switch -c <new-branch-name>
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253569Z 
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253656Z Or undo this operation with:
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253798Z 
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253878Z   git switch -
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7253978Z 
2026-04-13T02:17:36.7254164Z Turn off this advice by setting config variable advice.detachedHead to false

2026-04-13T02:17:51.8667284Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-13T02:17:51.8667820Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-13T02:17:51.8668243Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-13T02:17:51.8668614Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-13T02:17:51.8668923Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-13T02:17:51.8669162Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-13T02:17:51.8669341Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-13T02:22:44.0971595Z 
2026-04-13T02:22:44.0973197Z ◆ I see that `delete` is used as a method name in many operation interfaces. However, in TypeScript, `delete` is actually a reserved keyword BUT it can be used as a property name in an object/interface context. Let me check - is this actually an issue in this context?
2026-04-13T02:22:44.0974663Z   
2026-04-13T02:22:44.0975456Z   Actually, in TypeScript, `delete` can be used as a property/method name in an interface. It's `delete` as an expression that's a keyword (e.g., `delete obj.prop`). As an interface property, it's fine in TypeScript 4.x and later. Let me see what the guidelines say.
2026-04-13T02:22:44.0976297Z   
2026-04-13T02:22:44.0976842Z   Looking at the previous review for `@azure/arm-relationships` which flagged the `delete` reserved keyword issue, but that was for the old non-TypeSpec pattern. 
2026-04-13T02:22:44.0977429Z   
2026-04-13T02:22:44.0979624Z   In this TypeSpec-generated code, the `delete` method is a standard pattern across all operation groups, so it's consistent with how the generator handles these operations. The previous flag was specific to that older SDK, but this pattern appears to be the expected approach for TypeSpec-generated interfaces. I need to verify whether `delete` as a property name in TypeScript interfaces actually causes issues in practice, or if it's only problematic in specific contexts like destructuring. The real question is whether this SDK has the same reserved keyword problem that was flagged in arm-relationships, so I should check the guidelines to see what actually needs to be flagged.

The `V` prefix versus `Versions` prefix mismatch looks like a tooling bug. Since the CHANGELOG doesn't mention removing any `KnownVersions` values, the original `"V2024-11-01"` from version 1.0.0 should still be present alongside these new additions.

2026-04-13T02:22:44.2097353Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-13T02:17:53.699468942Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added. Target: 38084.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Create a review comment on a specific line of code in a pull request. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Review comment content in Markdown. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line.","type":["number","string"]},"path":{"description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js').
